title: Application for Deceased's Unnominated CPF Monies
text:
Application for Deceased's Unnominated CPF Monies - SupportGoWhere
Application for Deceased's Unnominated CPF Monies
Share link
Distribution of the un-nominated Central Provident Fund (CPF) monies of the Deceased Member in accordance with the relevant written law.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
CPF money of the deceased without a valid nomination is forwarded to the Public Trustee’s Office for administration
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

 The un-nominated Central Provident Fund (CPF) monies of the Deceased Member can be distributed according to the applicable intestacy laws.  

If the beneficiaries include minors, the Public Trustee’s Office can hold the money in trust for them until they reach the maturity age of 21 years old.

Who is this for?

Next-of-kin of deceased CPF member who wish to make a claim.

How to apply?

Apply online via the “Deceased’s un-nominated CPF / Baby Bonus / Edusave / PSEA” option at Public Trustee's Office (PTO) E-Services using your Singpass.  



If you are ineligible to apply for a Singpass, you may apply for a login ID and password here. Select 'Login' and choose 'Register as Individual' from the dropdown menu.




Please refer to Annex A (for Muslims) and Annex B (for non-Muslims) for documents required. 




If the deceased was a Muslim, you will need to apply for the Certificate of inheritance via Syariah Court .
 

You may attach the required documents in your online submission, send via e-services or post to PTO at the following address: 
 
  Public Trustee’s Office 
  The URA Centre (East Wing) 
  45 Maxwell Road, #07-11 
  Singapore 069118 




Please indicate the case reference number and NRIC of the deceased on your supporting documents




You will be assigned a case officer after you have filed your online application. Please inform your case officer if you do not possess some of the required documents. Your case officer will advise you on what to do after you have submitted your online application. 

Where can I find help?

View more details

Ministry of Law Hotline: 1800 225 5529
For overseas callers, please use +65 6225 5529




Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Ministry of Law Services Centre 
45 Maxwell Road 
#07-11 The URA Centre (East Wing) 
Singapore 069118

Scheme last updated 18 Mar 2026
